text: Galaxies spin too fast for visible matter alone — something unseen is holding them. Ever wondered why galaxies don't fly apart? It's all about rotation curves. As we measure their speeds, we find they’re moving way too fast — implying more mass is hiding out there. This unseen mass? That's dark matter — inferred, not directly detected. And yes, alternatives exist. But dark matter is still the leading model, explaining the universe's structure better than anything else. Curious about the universe? Hit like and follow for more mysteries!
